title: Blink an LED with Arduino
description: Learn to program a microcontroller by blinking an LED.

dialogue.text: Hey there! Orion here. Ready to do something a bit more exciting? Let's make that LED blink with a microcontroller.
All you need is an Arduino board, USB cable, a 220 ohm resistor, the LED from before, and a computer with the Arduino IDE installed. Hook the LED to pin 13 through the resistor.

options.text: Got it all wired up. What's next?

dialogue.text: Open the Arduino IDE, load the Blink example, select the correct port, and hit upload. The microcontroller will reset and start blinking the LED once every second.

options.text: Upload complete and the LED is blinking!

dialogue.text: Look at that! You just programmed a microcontroller. Keep experimenting with different patterns and sensors. This skill will come in handy for bigger projects.
